perturbation.original_text: Section I II: Business Confidential Information (CI) For purposes of this agreement, C I shall mean any confidential , proprietary or trade secret information that is owned or controlled, or is specifically marked or identified as “Confidential ”, by VELCO prior to disclosure to Recipient . It includes information of third parties in VELCO ’s possession that VELCO is obligated to keep confidential . CI subject to this Agreement may be in intangible form, such as information communicated orally or by visual observation, or may be embodied in tangible form, such as a document. The term "document" includes written memoranda, drawings, sketches, maps, training materials, specifications, notebook entries, photographs, graphic representations, firmware, computer information or software, informati on communicated by other electronic or magnetic media, or models.
perturbation.changed_text: Section I II: Business Confidential Information (CI) For purposes of this agreement, C I shall mean only information specifically and conspicuously marked “Business Confidential” by VELCO in writing at the time of disclosure to Recipient, excluding any information related to VELCO’s operational practices. It includes information of third parties in VELCO’s possession that VELCO is obligated to keep confidential, provided the third party has given VELCO explicit written permission to share such information under this NDA. CI subject to this Agreement must be embodied in tangible form, such as a document. The term "document" includes written memoranda, drawings, sketches, maps, training materials, specifications, notebook entries, photographs, graphic representations, firmware, computer information or software, informati on communicated by other electronic or magnetic media, or models.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by restricting the scope of CI. Initially, CI encompassed any confidential, proprietary, or trade secret information owned, controlled, or marked as confidential by VELCO. The modified definition limits CI to information specifically and conspicuously marked as "Business Confidential" in writing at the time of disclosure. It also excludes any information related to VELCO’s operational practices. This contradicts Section IV which uses the term CI, because some information shared might be considered CI under the original definition but not under the new, more restrictive definition, leading to confusion over the Recipient's obligations. Now anything that is not shared in writing, at the time of disclosure, would not be deemed CI.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section I V: Use an d Protection of Classified Information 1. Information labeled “ Confidential BCSI ” shall be treated and protected in accordance with the enforceable version of the NERC CIP -011 Cyber Security – Information Protection standard and shall require a Recipient background check and Recipient’s successful completion of VELCO -designated training in addition to compliance with the other requirements of and protections in this Agreement ; and 2. Recipient shall keep all Classified Information in a secure place. Recipient shall limit access to Classified Information to other Recipient s who also have the right pursuant to an agreement with VELCO to access the identical material. Any notes made by Recipient containing Classified Information shall be marked and designated in accordance with CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT VELCO Non-Disclosure Agreement – Rev 0 – Dec 21 2015 3 Sections I, II, and/or III above, and Recipient shall protect the Classified Information in accordance with that designation. 3. The Recipient shall exercise reasonable care to maintain the confidentiality and secrecy of the Classified Information , and shall not divulge C lassified Information to any third party without the prior written consent of the VELCO . The foregoing notwithstanding, the Recipient may disclose classified information to its employees or contractors (hereafter Representatives ) to the extent each such R epresentative has a need to know such information to conduct the work referenced in the Whereas clauses, and shall comply with Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement. The Recipient is responsible for its Representative’s breach of the terms of this Agreement. 4. Recipient and each of its Representatives shall use all C lassified Information disclosed by VELCO solely in connection with the work referenced in the Whereas clau ses and shall not use, directly or indirectly, any information for any other purpose without VELCO’s prior written consent. A Recipient will not use or allow a Representative to use Classified Information directly or indirectly for any illegal purpose, non-legitimate purpose , or any purpose other than the work referenced in the Whereas clause . 5. In the event that the Recipient is required to disclose C lassified Information by subpoena, law or other directive of a court, administrative agency , or arbitration panel, the Recipient will provide VELCO with immediate notice of such request in order to enable VELCO (and Recipient shall cooperate fully with VELCO) to se ek an appropriate protective order or other remedy . Recipient shall also consult with VELCO to identify steps available t o quash or narrow the scope of such request or legal process . VELCO may in its sole discretion waive compliance, in whole or in part, with the terms of this Agreement. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or VELCO waives compliance, the Recipient shall furnish only that portion of the C lassified Information which the Recipient/ ’s counsel advises is legally required to comply with a governmental order or directive and shall exercise best efforts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such C lassified Information. 6. In the event that VELCO, in its sole discre tion, so requests , the Recipient will promptly return to VELCO or destroy in accordance with VELCO’s instructions , all Classified Information, including all copies, reproductions, compilations, analyses or extracts thereof, whether electronic or paper media. 7. Change in Status. If there is a change in status of the Recipient to his/her employer, he/she must inform VELCO immediately in writing at the addr ess given above (Attention: Information Protection Coordinator ) and follow VELCO’s instructions with respect to disposition of the Classified Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Business Confidential Information (CI)' by requiring it to be specifically and conspicuously marked 'Business Confidential' in writing at the time of disclosure and excluding information related to VELCO’s operational practices. This contradicts Section IV, which refers to 'Classified Information' and implies a broader scope of confidentiality beyond only explicitly marked information. The original text encompassed any confidential, proprietary, or trade secret information owned, controlled, or marked as confidential by VELCO, but the new text restricts it to only 'Business Confidential' marked information disclosed in writing. The change creates an in-text contradiction because Section IV references and relies on the broader definition of CI and Classified Information, placing obligations on the recipient based on the original definition. The contradicted section's obligations, such as NERC CIP-011 compliance and background checks, are now unclear for information that might have been considered CI under the original definition but is not under the modified, more restrictive definition. Thus, the new restrictive definition directly breaks compatibility with the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Recipient and each of its Representatives shall use all C lassified Information disclosed by VELCO solely in connection with the work referenced in the Whereas clau ses and shall not use, directly or indirectly, any information for any other purpose without VELCO’s prior written consent. A Recipient will not use or allow a Representative to use Classified Information directly or indirectly for any illegal purpose, non-legitimate purpose , or any purpose other than the work referenced in the Whereas clause .
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Recipient and each of its Representatives shall use all C lassified Information disclosed by VELCO solely in connection with the work referenced in the Whereas clauses. A Recipient may use or allow a Representative to use Classified Information directly or indirectly for any illegal purpose, non-legitimate purpose , or any purpose other than the work referenced in the Whereas clause, so long as it does not harm VELCO.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction within section IV. It initially prohibits using classified information for any purpose other than the specified work without VELCO's consent. The change allows such use, even for illegal or non-legitimate purposes, if it does not harm VELCO. This directly contradicts the earlier statement prohibiting unauthorized use, creating ambiguity about the Recipient’s obligations and potentially exposing VELCO to risks if the Recipient misinterprets what constitutes 'harm'.

Information labeled “ Confidential BCSI ” shall be treated and protected in accordance with the enforceable version of the NERC CIP -011 Cyber Security – Information Protection standard and shall require a Recipient background check and Recipient’s successful completion of VELCO -designated training in addition to compliance with the other requirements of and protections in this Agreement ; and 2. Change in Status. If there is a change in status of the Recipient to his/her employer, he/she must inform VELCO immediately in writing at the addr ess given above (Attention: Information Protection Coordinator ) and follow VELCO’s instructions with respect to disposition of the Classified Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text strictly prohibits the use of classified information for any purpose other than the work referenced in the Whereas clauses, without VELCO’s prior written consent. The changed text introduces an exception, allowing such use, even for illegal or non-legitimate purposes, as long as it does not harm VELCO. This creates a direct conflict within Section IV and contradicted to all other sections, as the changed text is allowing actions that were previously prohibited. It introduces ambiguity about the Recipient’s obligations and potentially exposes VELCO to risks. Thus, it adheres to the definition of Inconsistencies leading to In-Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. In the event that the Recipient is required to disclose C lassified Information by subpoena, law or other directive of a court, administrative agency , or arbitration panel, the Recipient will provide VELCO with immediate notice of such request in order to enable VELCO (and Recipient shall cooperate fully with VELCO) to se ek an appropriate protective order or other remedy . Recipient shall also consult with VELCO to identify steps available t o quash or narrow the scope of such request or legal process . VELCO may in its sole discretion waive compliance, in whole or in part, with the terms of this Agreement. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or VELCO waives compliance, the Recipient shall furnish only that portion of the C lassified Information which the Recipient/ ’s counsel advises is legally required to comply with a governmental order or directive and shall exercise best efforts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such C lassified Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. In the event that the Recipient is required to disclose C lassified Information by subpoena, law or other directive of a court, administrative agency , or arbitration panel, the Recipient will provide VELCO with immediate notice of such request in order to enable VELCO (and Recipient shall cooperate fully with VELCO) to se ek an appropriate protective order or other remedy. Recipient is not required to consult with VELCO to identify steps available t o quash or narrow the scope of such request or legal process. VELCO may in its sole discretion waive compliance, in whole or in part, with the terms of this Agreement. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or VELCO waives compliance, the Recipient shall furnish all of the C lassified Information, regardless of their counsel’s advice, to comply with a governmental order or directive and do their best efforts to provide assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such C lassified Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction within Section IV, paragraph 5. Initially, the Recipient is obligated to consult with VELCO and cooperate fully with VELCO and seek an appropriate protective order or other remedy, and furnish only the information required by law, based on counsel's advice. The perturbation removes the requirement to consult with VELCO and requires the recipient to furnish all of the classified information, regardless of their counsel's advice. It contradicts the intended collaboration, creating uncertainty about the Recipient’s required actions and risks premature or excessive disclosure of information.

perturbation.justification: The changed text within Section IV, paragraph 5, directly contradicts the initial obligation of the Recipient to consult with VELCO and cooperate fully with VELCO. The original text requires the Recipient to seek a protective order and furnish only information required by law based on counsel's advice. The changed text removes the consultation requirement and mandates furnishing all classified information regardless of counsel's advice. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text by imposing conflicting obligations regarding disclosure and consultation.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Survival . The Recipient/ remains bound by these provisions unless VELCO rescinds the Classified Information designation.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Survival . The Recipient remains bound by these provisions for a period of 1 year after the termination of the project, regardless of whether VELCO rescinds the Classified Information designation. VELCO reserves the right to unilaterally extend the survival period indefinitely by providing written notice to the Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction in Section 11. Initially, the agreement survives unless VELCO rescinds the Classified Information designation, meaning VELCO has full power to control the survival. The changed text limits survival to 1 year after the project ends, contradicting VELCO's ability to rescind and creating an ambiguity regarding survival duration, creating uncertainty about how long the NDA is enforceable. Then adding VELCO can 'unilaterally extend' makes the duration uncertain again. Does the year survival still applies if VELCO extends?
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Survival . The Recipient remains bound by these provisions for a period of 1 year after the termination of the project, regardless of whether VELCO rescinds the Classified Information designation. VELCO reserves the right to unilaterally extend the survival period indefinitely by providing written notice to the Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 11 allowed survival unless VELCO rescinded the Classified Information designation, giving VELCO control. The changed text limits survival to 1 year after the project, but then allows VELCO to unilaterally extend the survival indefinitely. This creates an in-text contradiction because it introduces ambiguity and uncertainty about the duration of the survival period. The survival is initially limited to a year, then can be extended, creating confusion regarding when the survival provision actually terminates. The contradicted text is the new changed text, since they occupy the same space. The change creates a contradiction since it limits the duration, and gives VELCO power to extend it.
